meta.title: Shandong
meta.subtitle: Industry-cluster overview
meta.tagline: Shandong pairs national brand champions with one-product specialist towns: Haier and Weichai alongside the garlic, gold and fishing-tackle capitals. This is the cluster map of the province.
meta.methodology: Coordinates are from Google Places, converted from China's GCJ-02 datum to WGS-84 to sit correctly on the OpenStreetMap / CARTO basemap. Each pin marks a place's dominant manufacturing specialty drawn from current sourcing guides; clusters are indicative, not exclusive, and shift over time. The map covers all 16 of Shandong's prefecture-level cities; the Bohai Rim overview maps Shandong's coastal majors within its wider region.

clusters.note: Home-appliance capital — Haier, Hisense; plus tires, trains (CRRC Sifang) and Tsingtao beer.
Garment and knitwear wholesale base; one of north China's biggest clothing markets.
False-eyelash capital — a large share of the world's lashes and hair products.
Diesel engines (Weichai), agricultural machinery (Lovol) and acoustics/MEMS (Goertek); kites.
China's vegetable capital — the largest greenhouse-vegetable base and wholesale market.
Heavy-truck capital (Sinotruk); heavy industry, transformers and software.
Ceramics base — building and art ceramics; petrochemicals and new materials.
Lampwork and art-glass capital; daily glassware and inner-painted bottles.
China's wine capital (Changyu); autos and parts, electronics and seafood.
China's gold capital — gold mining and processing.
World fishing-tackle capital — rods and reels; tires (Triangle), swimwear and marine industry.
Northern China's wholesale-commodity capital — the 'Yiwu of the north'; vast logistics.
Plywood and engineered-board capital — much of China's plywood output.
Construction and mining machinery (Shantui bulldozers); coal and engineering equipment.
World garlic capital — the dominant garlic production and export base.
Semi-trailer and special-vehicle capital — China's largest commercial-vehicle base.
Shengli oilfield and the Yellow River Delta — petrochemicals, rubber and oil equipment.
'Solar Valley' — solar water heaters and renewable-energy equipment (Himin); HVAC.
Coal chemicals and machine tools; a growing lithium-battery and new-energy base.
Textiles, fibreglass and vehicles; transformers and tourism (Mount Tai).
Steel (Rizhao Steel) and a major bulk port; pulp, paper and tires.
Bearings (Linqing) and non-ferrous metals; auto parts.
Weiqiao — the world's largest aluminium producer and one of the largest cotton-textile makers.
Commercial-kitchen and stainless-steel kitchenware capital.
Hanfu and performance-costume capital; coffins and rosewood furniture — a famous e-commerce county.
Peony capital (oil and tea) and a fine-chemical and pharmaceutical base.

orientation.v: Qingdao appliances (Haier, Hisense) and beer, Yantai wine (Changyu), Weihai fishing tackle, Weifang's Weichai engines and Goertek acoustics.
Jinan heavy trucks (Sinotruk), Jining mining and construction machinery (Shantui), Rizhao steel, Dongying's Shengli oilfield and petrochemicals.
Shouguang the vegetable capital, Jinxiang the world's garlic capital, Heze peony — the plain's food-processing belt.
Zhaoyuan gold, Binzhou's Weiqiao (the world's largest aluminium maker) and cotton textiles, Linqing bearings, Boxing kitchenware.
Liangshan trailers, Pingdu eyelashes, Cao County Hanfu and costumes, Dezhou's Solar Valley, Zibo and Boshan ceramics and glass, Linyi's wholesale-trade city.

notes: Shandong is one of China's biggest manufacturing and agricultural provinces, unusual for how it pairs heavyweight national brands (Haier, Hisense, Weichai, Sinotruk, Tsingtao, Changyu, Weiqiao) with intensely specialised towns.
The coast carries the brand champions and ports (Qingdao, Yantai, Weihai, Rizhao), while the inland plain runs on agriculture, food processing, machinery and metals.

The specialist towns are remarkable: Shouguang grows the nation's vegetables, Jinxiang most of its garlic, Zhaoyuan mines its gold, Weihai makes the world's fishing tackle, Pingdu its false eyelashes, Cao County its Hanfu.

A pin marks a place's dominant specialty, not its only one; the big cities make many things. The Bohai Rim overview maps Shandong's coastal majors alongside Beijing, Tianjin, Hebei and Liaoning; this map zooms in on the whole province.
